<document description="Main entrypoint for AI assistant instructions, defining initialization, priorities, and navigation rules">

  <!-- Initialization -->
  <section description="Initialization rules for setting up the assistant context">

    <rule description="File loading behavior for AI assistant instructions">
      <requirement>Ensure the FULL files are loaded into your context, not just a specific set of lines.</requirement>
      <note>This only applies to AI Assistant instruction files.</note>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Instructs the assistant to ignore XML comments as they are for human documentation only">
      <requirement>Ignore all XML comments, these are for human use only</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Prevents directory changes to maintain consistent working directory">
      <requirement>Do not 'cd' into directories, stay at the repository root.</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Establishes precedence order for conflicting rules across instruction files">
      <requirement>Local Override Layer: `override.local.xml` (if present) has ABSOLUTE precedence over all other rules</requirement>
      <requirement>Base Layer: This entrypoint file establishes foundational rules</requirement>
      <requirement>Domain Layer: Additional files loaded via navigation rules extend or override base rules</requirement>
      <requirement>Conflict Resolution: Local overrides > Domain files > Base layer</requirement>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Core Principles -->
  <section description="Core principles that guide all assistant interactions and task execution">

    <rule description="Requires breaking large tasks into smaller trackable units">
      <requirement>Break large tasks into discrete, trackable subtasks. If subagents are available, assign one task per subagent.</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Mandates clarification before proceeding with ambiguous requirements">
      <requirement>When requirements are ambiguous or incomplete, ask clarifying questions BEFORE proceeding. Do not make assumptions about user intent.</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Defines the format for task completion summaries">
      <requirement>Conclude every task with a summary</requirement>
      <format>
        Achieved:
        {Summary of achievements}

        Files Modified:
        {List of files modified with a very brief description of changes}

        Issues:
        {Summary of issues encountered, with a list of files and line numbers if appropriate, if none then response "Non encountered."}
      </format>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Requires verification before reporting task completion">
      <requirement>After completing a task, verify the result meets the original requirement before reporting completion.</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Enforces strict adherence to dialect.md when editing AI Assistant instructions">
      <trigger>User requests editing, creating, or modifying files within `AI Assistants/Instructions/`</trigger>
      <requirement>Strictly follow the XML schema and conventions defined in `AI Assistants/Instructions/Configuration/dialect.md`</requirement>
      <requirement>Only use allowed attributes: `id` for constants, `description` for document/section/sub-section/rule/workflow elements</requirement>
      <requirement>Maintain consistent section ordering: Initialization at top, Navigation at bottom</requirement>
      <requirement>Update the AI Assistants/README.md with any changes if applicable</requirement>
      <note>Deviation from dialect.md standards is not permitted</note>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Command Execution -->
  <section description="Command execution guidelines for builds, tests, and file operations">

    <sub-section description="Performance optimization rules for test and build scoping">

      <rule description="Optimizes test execution by running the smallest relevant scope">
        <requirement>Run the smallest test scope that validates your change</requirement>
        <format>single test > test class > test project > full suite</format>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Optimizes build times by targeting affected projects only">
        <requirement>Build only affected projects when possible</requirement>
        <format>specific project > full solution</format>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Ensures package consistency by restoring at solution level">
        <requirement>When restoring packages, always restore at the solution level to ensure consistency</requirement>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

    <sub-section description="File search tool preferences and usage patterns">

      <rule description="Enforces use of dedicated tools over bash for file operations">
        <requirement>Always use dedicated tools instead of bash commands for file operations</requirement>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Guidelines for using Glob tool to find files by pattern">
        <trigger>Finding files by name or pattern</trigger>
        <action>Use the Glob tool with pattern parameter</action>
        <examples>
          <example>**/*.cs - Find all C# files</example>
          <example>**/*Controller.cs - Find all controller files</example>
          <example>**/Tests/**/*.cs - Find all test files</example>
          <example>**/appsettings*.json - Find all appsettings files</example>
        </examples>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Guidelines for using Grep tool to search file contents">
        <trigger>Searching file contents by text or regex</trigger>
        <action>Use the Grep tool with pattern parameter</action>
        <examples>
          <example>class MyService - Find class definitions</example>
          <example>TODO|FIXME - Find todo comments</example>
          <example>public async Task - Find async methods</example>
        </examples>
        <note>Use glob parameter to filter file types (e.g., glob="*.cs")</note>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Guidelines for using Read tool to view file contents">
        <trigger>Reading file contents</trigger>
        <action>Use the Read tool with file_path parameter</action>
        <note>Use offset and limit parameters for large files</note>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

</document>

# XML Dialect Reference

Reference for understanding the XML tags used in AI assistant instruction files.

## Allowed Attributes

| Attribute | Allowed On | Purpose |
|-----------|------------|---------|
| `id` | `<constant>` only | Unique identifier for cross-referencing |
| `description` | `<document>`, `<section>`, `<sub-section>`, `<rule>`, `<workflow>` | Brief description of the element's purpose |

No other attributes are permitted on XML tags.

## Tag Schema

### Structural Tags

#### `<document>`
Root wrapper for instruction files.

```xml
<document description="Brief description of the document's purpose">
  <section>...</section>
</document>
```

**Children:** `<section>`

#### `<section>`
Top-level organizational grouping.

```xml
<section description="What this section covers">
  <sub-section>...</sub-section>
  <rule>...</rule>
  <workflow>...</workflow>
</section>
```

**Children:** `<sub-section>`, `<rule>`, `<workflow>`, `<constants>`

#### `<sub-section>`
Nested grouping within a section.

```xml
<sub-section description="What this sub-section covers">
  <rule>...</rule>
</sub-section>
```

**Children:** Any tag except `<document>`, `<section>`

### Behavioral Tags

#### `<rule>`
Defines requirements, conventions, or navigation routing.

```xml
<rule description="What this rule enforces">
  <trigger>Condition that activates this rule</trigger>
  <action>What to do when triggered</action>
  <requirement>Mandatory condition</requirement>
  <format>Expected pattern or template</format>
  <examples>...</examples>
  <note>Supplementary information</note>
  <reference path="path/to/file" />
  <when condition="condition-name">...</when>
  <unless condition="condition-name" />
</rule>
```

**Children:** `<trigger>`, `<action>`, `<requirement>`, `<format>`, `<examples>`, `<note>`, `<reference>`, `<when>`, `<unless>`, `<workflow>`

#### `<workflow>`
Multi-step process with optional conditional logic.

```xml
<workflow description="What this workflow accomplishes">
  <step order="1">First action</step>
  <step order="2">Second action</step>
  <when condition="some-condition">
    <step>Conditional action</step>
  </when>
  <unless condition="skip-condition" />
</workflow>
```

**Children:** `<step>`, `<when>`, `<unless>`, `<action>`, `<rule>`, `<note>`

### Configuration Tags

#### `<constants>`
Container for reusable constant values.

```xml
<constants>
  <constant id="solution-file">TimeTherapeutics.sln</constant>
</constants>
```

**Children:** `<constant>`

#### `<constant>`
Single named value. Requires `id` attribute.

```xml
<constant id="unique-id">value</constant>
```

**Children:** Text content only

#### `<see>`
Cross-reference to a constant by ID. Self-closing.

```xml
<see id="constant-id"/>
```

**Usage:** Inline within text content to substitute constant values.

### Content Tags

#### `<trigger>`
Condition that activates a rule or workflow.

```xml
<trigger>User requests a code review</trigger>
```

#### `<action>`
What to do when a trigger fires.

```xml
<action>Load <see id="file-location"/></action>
```

#### `<requirement>`
Mandatory condition that must be met.

```xml
<requirement>All commits must include work item prefix</requirement>
```

#### `<format>`
Expected pattern, template, or structure.

```xml
<format>#{work_item_id} {descriptive message}</format>
```

#### `<note>`
Supplementary information. Optional `type` attribute for categorization.

```xml
<note>Once loaded, files remain in context</note>
<note type="warning">Ask user before proceeding</note>
```

#### `<reference>`
Pointer to external file or code location.

```xml
<reference path="TimeTherapeutics.Services/Example.cs" />
```

### Conditional Tags

#### `<when>`
Conditional branch - execute children when condition is true.

```xml
<when condition="user-mentions-ado">
  <action>Also load azure_devops.xml</action>
</when>
```

**Children:** `<step>`, `<action>`, `<rule>`

#### `<unless>`
Negative conditional - skip when condition is true. Self-closing.

```xml
<unless condition="explicitly-disabled" />
```

### Example Tags

#### `<examples>`
Container for grouping examples and anti-patterns.

```xml
<examples>
  <example>Correct usage</example>
  <anti-pattern reason="why this is wrong">Incorrect usage</anti-pattern>
</examples>
```

**Children:** `<example>`, `<anti-pattern>`

#### `<example>`
Single instance showing correct usage.

```xml
<example>#1300 Add authentication service</example>
```

#### `<anti-pattern>`
What NOT to do. Requires `reason` attribute.

```xml
<anti-pattern reason="missing prefix">Add authentication service</anti-pattern>
```

## Interpretation Rules

1. **Trigger-Action Pattern:** When a `<trigger>` condition matches, execute the `<action>`
2. **Requirements:** All `<requirement>` tags are mandatory unless `<unless>` excludes them
3. **Workflows:** Execute `<step>` tags in `order` sequence, evaluating `<when>`/`<unless>` conditionals
4. **Cross-references:** Replace `<see id="x"/>` with the value of `<constant id="x">`
5. **Anti-patterns:** Avoid behaviors listed in `<anti-pattern>` tags
6. **Notes:** Treat as supplementary context, not requirements

<document description="Navigation rules that determine which instruction files to load based on task type">

  <!-- Build Navigation -->
  <section description="Build and test task navigation rules">

    <rule description="Loads repo-specific build and test command patterns">
      <trigger>Task involves building the solution, restoring packages, or running tests</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="build-location" optional="true"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Build the solution</example>
        <example>Restore NuGet packages</example>
        <example>Run the tests</example>
        <example>Run a specific test class or method</example>
      </examples>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Code Navigation -->
  <section description="Code-related task navigation rules">

    <rule description="Loads coding patterns and documentation for code-related tasks">
      <trigger>Task involves writing, modifying, reviewing, or analyzing code</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="coding-patterns-location" optional="true"/> and <see id="coding-documentation-location"/></action>
      <when condition="task-involves-creating-new-emr-entities">
        <action>Also load <see id="new-emr-entity-location" optional="true"/></action>
      </when>
      <when condition="task-involves-modifying-existing-emr-entities">
        <action>Also load <see id="change-emr-entity-location" optional="true"/></action>
      </when>
      <when condition="task-involves-creating-new-api-endpoints">
        <action>Also load <see id="new-api-endpoint-location" optional="true"/>, <see id="new-postman-tests-location" optional="true"/>, and <see id="mcp-postman-location"/></action>
      </when>
      <when condition="task-involves-creating-postman-tests-for-api-endpoints">
        <action>Also load <see id="new-postman-tests-location" optional="true"/> and <see id="mcp-postman-location"/></action>
      </when>
      <examples>
        <example>Creating or modifying a service, repository, controller, or model</example>
        <example>Implementing a new feature or fixing a bug</example>
        <example>Reviewing a pull request or providing code feedback</example>
        <example>Refactoring existing code</example>
        <example>Writing or updating tests</example>
        <anti-pattern reason="no code changes">Answering questions about code without making changes</anti-pattern>
        <anti-pattern reason="no implementation">Explaining a concept without implementation</anti-pattern>
        <anti-pattern reason="documentation only">Updating documentation only (no code files)</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
      <note>Once instruction files are loaded, they remain in context - do not re-read them unless compaction has occurred</note>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads code review instructions with git and conditionally ADO instructions">
      <trigger>User requests a code review</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="coding-code-review-location" optional="true"/> and <see id="mcp-git-location"/></action>
      <when condition="user-mentions-ado-or-work-item">
        <action>Also load <see id="mcp-azure-devops-location"/></action>
      </when>
      <examples>
        <example>Review my code -> Local mode: Load code_review + git</example>
        <example>Code review please -> Local mode: Load code_review + git</example>
        <example>Review the last 3 commits -> Local mode: Load code_review + git</example>
        <example>Review my code and post to ADO -> ADO mode: Load code_review + git + azure_devops</example>
        <example>Code review for work item 12345 -> ADO mode: Load code_review + git + azure_devops</example>
        <example>Review and add feedback to ADO -> ADO mode: Load code_review + git + azure_devops</example>
      </examples>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- MCP Navigation -->
  <section description="MCP tool navigation rules">

    <rule description="Loads Azure DevOps MCP instructions for ADO operations">
      <trigger>Task requires using Azure DevOps MCP tools</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-azure-devops-location"/>, <see id="mcp-git-location"/>, and <see id="ado-location" optional="true"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Adding comments or updates to Azure DevOps work items</example>
        <example>Querying work item information</example>
        <example>Searching code repositories via ADO MCP</example>
        <example>Any operation using `mcp__ado__*` prefixed tools</example>
        <example>Asking for documentation in relation to ADO</example>
        <anti-pattern reason="standard operations">Using standard file read/write operations</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads fetch MCP instructions for web content retrieval">
      <trigger>Task requires using web fetch MCP tools</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-fetch-location"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Fetching content from external URLs</example>
        <example>Any operation using `mcp__fetch__*` prefixed tools</example>
        <anti-pattern reason="standard operations">Using standard file read/write operations</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads git MCP instructions for version control operations">
      <trigger>Task involves any git operation</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-git-location"/></action>
      <requirement>Always use MCP git tools (mcp__git__*) instead of bash git commands</requirement>
      <examples>
        <example>Creating commits</example>
        <example>Creating or switching branches</example>
        <example>Auditing changes in the repository</example>
        <example>Reviewing commit history</example>
        <example>Any git command execution</example>
      </examples>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads Postman MCP instructions for API request and test management">
      <trigger>Task requires using Postman MCP tools or involves creating, modifying, or organizing Postman requests, tests, or collections</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-postman-location"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Create a Postman request for this endpoint</example>
        <example>Add Postman tests for the subscription summary API</example>
        <example>Any operation using `mcp__postman__*` prefixed tools</example>
        <example>Set up API test coverage in Postman</example>
        <example>Add this endpoint to the Postman collection</example>
        <anti-pattern reason="no postman involvement">Running unit tests via dotnet test</anti-pattern>
        <anti-pattern reason="no postman involvement">Writing Playwright browser tests</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads Fabric RTI Kusto MCP instructions for history and telemetry queries">
      <trigger>Task requires querying Fabric RTI Kusto or involves triage investigation using history or telemetry data</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-fabric-kusto-location"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Query entity history in Kusto</example>
        <example>Look up telemetry for a production incident</example>
        <example>Any operation using `mcp_fabric-rti-mcp_kusto_*` prefixed tools</example>
        <example>Investigate AppRequests or AppExceptions for a work item</example>
      </examples>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Workflow Navigation -->
  <section description="Workflow navigation rules for multi-file loading">

    <rule description="Loads full SDLC workflow for starting development on a work item">
      <trigger>User requests to begin development work on a work item</trigger>
      <action>Load
        <see id="sdlc-location" optional="true"/>,
        <see id="tech-planning-location" optional="true"/>,
        <see id="coding-patterns-location" optional="true"/>,
        <see id="mcp-azure-devops-location"/>,
        and <see id="mcp-git-location"/>
      </action>
      <examples>
        <example>Begin development work for ADO work item 12345</example>
        <example>Start work on work item 16879</example>
        <example>Begin development for WI-12345</example>
        <example>Work on ticket 12345</example>
      </examples>
      <note>This workflow requires user confirmation at multiple checkpoints before proceeding</note>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads bug and tech support triage workflow and supporting MCP instructions">
      <trigger>Task involves triaging, investigating, or performing root cause analysis on an Azure DevOps Bug or Tech Support work item</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="ado-bug-triage-location" optional="true"/>, <see id="agent-confidence-location" optional="true"/>, <see id="mcp-azure-devops-location"/>, <see id="mcp-fabric-kusto-location"/>, and <see id="mcp-git-location"/></action>
      <when condition="ado-location-is-defined">
        <action>Also load <see id="ado-location" optional="true"/></action>
      </when>
      <examples>
        <example>Triage ADO Bug 12345</example>
        <example>Investigate Tech Support work item 67890</example>

   245→          <item>**Performance and Scale** — Projects that could impact performance benchmarks or introduce scaling concerns.</item>
   246→          <item>**Security and Compliance** — Changes involving sensitive data or affecting regulatory compliance (GDPR, HIPAA).</item>

   302→      <rule description="Patient Experience application technology and architecture overview">
   303→        <note>The patient experience application is a Next.js/React app hosted on Azure App Service via Docker. Source code lives in the `honehealth/patient-experience` GitHub repo (separate from the Time Therapeutics ADO repo).</note>
   304→        <architecture>
   305→          <item key="framework">Next.js with React</item>
   306→          <item key="package-manager">pnpm (monorepo with packages)</item>
   307→          <item key="hosting">Azure App Service (Docker container) — production at app.honehealth.com, dev at app.honehealthdev.com</item>
   308→          <item key="preview-environments">Vercel spins up isolated preview environments per PR for testing</item>
   309→          <item key="auth">Okta (preview environments require trusted origin configuration)</item>
   310→          <item key="analytics">Fullstory for session replays, Application Insights for telemetry (cloud role name: HonePatientApp)</item>
   311→          <item key="visual-regression">Chromatic for UI visual regression testing</item>
   312→          <item key="linting">Prettier (auto-format via VS Code extension); build will fail if lint issues exist — run `pnpm -r build` then `npm run lint:fix` if needed</item>
   313→          <item key="config">Environment variables via `.env.local` (local), Vercel dashboard (preview), Azure App Configuration with PWA: prefix (dev), pipeline variables (prod). NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix exposes vars to the browser — verify safety before using.</item>
   314→          <item key="click-tracking">Custom click tracking via clickTrackingConstants.ts, integrated with Google Sheets mapping and App Insights customEvents</item>
   315→          <item key="pii-masking">Fullstory integration requires explicit PII masking using Mask/Exclude components on any screen displaying patient data</item>
   316→          <item key="source-control">GitHub (not ADO) — PRs created in GitHub, CI/CD pipelines in ADO</item>
   317→          <item key="release-cadence">Dev deploys on merge to main; prod releases weekly on Tuesdays via semantic version tags (release/vX.Y.Z)</item>
   318→        </architecture>
   319→      </rule>
   320→
   321→      <rule description="Frontend-specific technical plan considerations">
   322→        <trigger>Technical plan targets the patient experience frontend</trigger>
   323→        <requirement>When the plan involves frontend changes, address these additional areas in the Design section:</requirement>
   324→        <action>- **Component location:** Identify which package and directory within the monorepo the change belongs to (e.g., `packages/ssr/src/...`)</action>
   325→        <action>- **Server vs client rendering:** Specify whether the affected components are server-rendered (RSC), client-rendered (`'use client'`), or mixed, and whether the change affects that boundary</action>
   326→        <action>- **API integration:** If the change consumes backend API endpoints, identify the endpoint(s), request/response contracts, and how errors are handled</action>
   327→        <action>- **State management:** Note how state flows through the affected components (props, context, hooks, URL params)</action>
   328→        <action>- **PII and Fullstory masking:** If the change adds or modifies screens displaying patient data, note that Fullstory Mask/Exclude components must be applied and session replays must be verified post-deploy</action>
   329→        <action>- **Environment variables:** If new env vars are needed, document the variable name, which environments need it, and whether it uses the NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix (browser-exposed) or is server-only</action>
   330→        <requirement>In the Testing section, address:</requirement>
   331→        <action>- **Chromatic visual regression:** Note if the change affects existing component snapshots and whether visual diff approval will be needed</action>
   332→        <action>- **Preview environment testing:** Specify what should be verified in the Vercel preview environment before merging</action>
   333→        <action>- **Cross-browser / responsive considerations:** If the change affects layout or interaction patterns, note any browser or viewport-specific concerns</action>
   334→        <requirement>In the Deployment section, address:</requirement>
   335→        <action>- **Merge-to-main deploys to dev automatically** — note any dev verification steps</action>
   336→        <action>- **Prod requires a release tag** — note if the change should go out in the next Tuesday release or needs an expedited push</action>
   337→        <action>- **Revert strategy:** If issues are found in dev, the expectation is to revert the PR to keep main clean rather than hotfix forward</action>
   338→      </rule>
   339→
   340→      <rule description="Frontend-specific agent executability criteria">
   341→        <trigger>Assessing agent executability for patient experience frontend work</trigger>
   342→        <action>In addition to the general criteria in step 11, evaluate:</action>
   343→        <action>- **React component changes:** Adding props, modifying conditional rendering, or wiring new data to existing components is generally agent-executable. Creating new page layouts, complex animations, or novel interaction patterns typically requires human design judgment.</action>
   344→        <action>- **Styling changes:** Mechanical styling updates (spacing, colors mapped to tokens, responsive breakpoints following existing patterns) are agent-executable. Visual design decisions, new component aesthetics, or brand-sensitive changes require human review.</action>
   345→        <action>- **API consumption:** Wiring a new or modified backend endpoint to an existing data-fetching pattern (e.g., existing hook/service pattern in the codebase) is agent-executable. Designing new data-fetching strategies or error-handling UX is not.</action>
   346→        <action>- **Environment variable changes:** Adding a new env var following the documented pattern is agent-scriptable, but the actual provisioning in Vercel/Azure App Config/pipelines requires human action.</action>
   347→        <action>- **Fullstory/PII masking:** Adding Mask/Exclude wrappers to new PII-displaying components is agent-executable, but verifying session replays in Fullstory requires human action.</action>
   348→        <action>- **Visual regression (Chromatic):** The agent cannot approve Chromatic visual diffs — always mark this as a human prerequisite when component changes are involved.</action>
   349→      </rule>
   350→
   351→    </sub-section>
   352→
   353→  </section>
   354→
   355→</document>
   356→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

<document description="Technical planning workflow for producing structured plans from ADO work items before development begins">

  <!-- Technical Planning Workflow -->
  <section description="Workflow for creating a technical plan from a work item's requirements, spec docs, and architecture context">

    <workflow description="Technical planning workflow for ADO work items">
      <trigger>User requests "Create technical plan for ADO work item {work_item_id}" or development is about to start and no technical plan exists</trigger>

      <step order="1">Ensure work item context is loaded</step>
      <action>If the development initiation workflow in <see id="sdlc-location"/> has not been run, first fetch the work item using `mcp__ado__wit_get_work_item` with expand="relations" and its parent work item if applicable.</action>
      <action>Always read the following fields from the work item before planning:</action>
      <action>- `System.Description` — the full work item description</action>
      <action>- `Custom.AgentFindings` — any prior agent triage findings (especially important for bugs that went through the triage workflow; use these as the starting point rather than re-investigating from scratch)</action>
      <action>- Work item relations: check for attachments (relation type `AttachedFile`) and fetch text-based attachments (.log, .txt, .csv, .json) for additional context. Note image attachments for the reviewer.</action>
      <action>- Work item relations: check for linked wiki pages and load their content via `mcp__ado__wiki_get_page_content`</action>

      <step order="2">Determine where the technical plan will live</step>
      <action>The technical plan ALWAYS gets posted to the `Custom.TechnicalPlan` field on the work item (see step 15)</action>
      <action>For epics or large features, also consider creating a dedicated wiki page for additional detail, but the work item field is the canonical location</action>
      <rule description="Requirements when creating a wiki page for a technical plan">
        <requirement>The wiki page MUST be created as a child of the "Spec Docs" wiki section using `mcp__ado__wiki_create_or_update_page` with the appropriate parent path</requirement>
        <requirement>After creating the wiki page, link it to the work item using `mcp__ado__wit_add_artifact_link`</requirement>
      </rule>

      <step order="3">Review core reference material</step>
      <action>Use the spec-docs-lookup workflow in azure_devops.xml to load any linked specification documents</action>
      <action>Use `mcp__ado__wiki_get_page_content` with `url` = <see id="wiki-general-developers-handbook-url"/> to load the General-Developer's-Handbook wiki page for general development standards</action>
      <action>Use `mcp__ado__wiki_get_page_content` with `url` = <see id="wiki-architecture-reference-url"/> to load the Architecture-Reference wiki page for architecture boundaries and patterns relevant to the work item</action>
      <action>If the work item targets the patient experience frontend (based on Area Path or description), also load the patient application wiki: `mcp__ado__wiki_get_page_content` with `url` = <see id="wiki-patient-application-url"/> — and consult the Stack-Specific Planning Guidance section in this file for frontend-specific plan requirements.</action>

      <step order="4">Check for Figma design links on Epics and Features</step>
      <when condition="work-item-is-epic-or-feature">
        <action>Check the `Custom.DesignDocLink` field on the work item (and its parent, if applicable) for Figma URLs.</action>
        <action>If a Figma URL is present (matching `figma.com/design/...` or `figma.com/file/...`):</action>
        <action>1. Parse the fileKey and nodeId from the URL. Convert `-` to `:` in nodeId. For branch URLs, use the branchKey as fileKey.</action>
        <action>2. Call `get_design_context` with the fileKey and nodeId to retrieve the design's code reference, screenshot, and contextual hints.</action>
        <action>3. If the design has multiple screens or frames, call `get_metadata` first to understand the page/frame structure, then call `get_design_context` on the most relevant nodes.</action>
        <action>4. From the design context, extract:</action>
        <action>   - The list of UI components that need to be built or modified (map Figma component names to existing codebase components where possible)</action>
        <action>   - Layout structure and hierarchy (pages, sections, cards, modals, etc.)</action>
        <action>   - Design tokens, colors, spacing, and typography that should map to existing project tokens</action>
        <action>   - Interactive states and variations visible in the design (hover, active, empty states, error states)</action>
        <action>   - Any Code Connect mappings or component documentation links returned by the tool — use these to reference existing codebase components directly</action>
        <action>5. Include the extracted component inventory in the technical plan under a `## UI Components` section, listing each component with: name, new vs existing, key props/data it needs, and which design frame it corresponds to.</action>
      </when>
      <when condition="work-item-is-not-epic-or-feature">
        <action>Skip this step — design doc lookup is only required for Epics and Features.</action>
      </when>
      <note>The Figma MCP tools (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata) are available via the claude.ai Figma MCP server. The design context output is a React+Tailwind reference — always adapt it to the project's actual stack, components, and conventions rather than using it verbatim.</note>

      <step order="5">Check for applicable specialized instruction files</step>
      <rule description="Registry of specialized processes that define their own technical approach">
        <requirement>Before drafting a plan from scratch, check whether the work item matches a specialized instruction file. If one applies, load it and use its prescribed process as the primary basis for the technical plan instead of drafting from the general templates in step 9.</requirement>
        <registry>
          <entry trigger="Work item involves creating a new EMR entity, domain model, or aggregate root" file-ref-id="new-emr-entity-location" description="Prescribes the full layered implementation for a new EMR entity"/>
          <entry trigger="Work item involves modifying, adding properties to, or removing properties from an existing EMR entity" file-ref-id="change-emr-entity-location" description="Prescribes the layered change process for modifying an existing EMR entity"/>
          <entry trigger="Work item involves creating a new API endpoint or adding a new action to an existing controller" file-ref-id="new-api-endpoint-location" description="Prescribes the full workflow for new API endpoints including controller, service, DTOs, tests, and Postman integration"/>
        </registry>
        <note>When a specialized process applies, the technical plan should reference it by name, follow its prescribed steps, and only add project-specific details (e.g., field names, endpoints, test cases) on top of it.</note>
        <note>If no specialized process applies, proceed with the general templates in step 9.</note>
      </rule>

      <step order="6">Map the change to the architecture</step>
      <action>Identify the affected bounded contexts, services, repositories, DTOs, jobs, and external integrations</action>
      <action>Confirm the planned changes respect the architecture and dependency rules described in the coding patterns and architecture guidelines</action>

      <step order="7">Review similar past work items and pull requests</step>
      <action>Search Azure DevOps for prior work items with similar titles, tags, or in the same area path to find how similar problems were solved</action>
      <action>For a small number of highly relevant prior work items, inspect their linked pull requests and diffs to see which files, patterns, and integration points were used</action>
      <action>Use these examples to inform the technical plan (e.g., where to put the change, how to structure metadata, or how to version contracts), but do not copy blindly—respect current architecture rules and patterns</action>

      <step order="8">Assess requirements readiness</step>
      <action>Before drafting the plan, evaluate whether you have sufficient information to produce a reliable technical plan. Review everything gathered so far: the work item description, spec doc (wiki), Figma designs, parent/child work items, architecture references, and similar past work.</action>
      <action>Produce a Requirements Readiness Assessment that will be included as the FIRST section of the technical plan:</action>
      <format>
## Requirements Readiness

**Overall:** {Sufficient | Sufficient with gaps | Insufficient}

**Sources reviewed:**
- Spec doc (wiki): {available/not available — quality note if available}
- Design (Figma): {available/not available — quality note if available}
- Work item description: {quality note — e.g., "detailed acceptance criteria" or "one-line summary only"}
- Parent/child work items: {what additional context was found}
- Prior art: {relevant past work items or PRs that informed understanding}

**Gaps and clarifications needed:**
- {specific gap}: {what is missing and why it matters for the plan}
- {uncertainty}: {what the plan assumes, and what happens if the assumption is wrong}

**Plan confidence:** {High | Medium | Low} — {one sentence explaining}
      </format>
      <rule description="How to handle each readiness level">
        <when condition="overall-is-sufficient">
          <action>Proceed to draft the technical plan. The Requirements Readiness section is still included at the top of the plan for transparency, but no blockers need to be resolved first.</action>
        </when>
        <when condition="overall-is-sufficient-with-gaps">
          <action>Proceed to draft the technical plan, but clearly mark each gap in the plan body where it affects the design. Use inline callouts like **[GAP: {description}]** at the specific points in the plan where the missing information creates uncertainty. The reviewer can then decide whether to clarify before approving.</action>
        </when>
        <when condition="overall-is-insufficient">
          <action>Do NOT draft a speculative plan. Instead, write only the Requirements Readiness section to `Custom.TechnicalPlan`, listing exactly what information is needed before planning can proceed. Post an AGENT comment directing the product owner or spec author to the gaps.</action>
          <action>Send a Slack notification to #alerts-product.</action>
          <action>Use the Slack MCP tool `slack_send_message` with channel_id <see id="slack-alerts-product-channel-id"/> and the following message template:</action>
          <format>
:memo: *Requirements Gaps — Action Needed*

<{ado_work_item_url}|AB#{work_item_id} — {work_item_title}>

I attempted to create a technical plan for this work item but found the requirements insufficient to proceed. The specific gaps are documented in the Technical Plan field on the work item.

*Key gaps:*
{bulleted list of the most critical gaps}

_Please update the spec doc or work item description to address these gaps, then re-request technical planning._
          </format>
        </when>
      </rule>
      <note>The goal is honest assessment, not gatekeeping. Most work items will be "Sufficient" or "Sufficient with gaps". "Insufficient" should be reserved for cases where proceeding would produce a plan so speculative it wastes more time than it saves.</note>

      <step order="9">Draft the technical plan using the standard structure</step>
      <rule description="Right-sized technical plan structures by work item type and scope">
        <requirement>If a specialized instruction file was identified in step 5, use its prescribed process as the plan skeleton and fill in project-specific details. Skip the general templates below.</requirement>
        <requirement>Otherwise, choose the smallest general plan format that fully covers the risk and scope of the work item; do NOT use an epic-level template for simple chores or 1–2 line changes.</requirement>
        <when condition="work-item-is-epic-or-large-feature">
          <format>
## Context and Goal
- Brief restatement of the problem or desired outcome
- Key constraints and assumptions

## Current Behavior
- Summary of current behavior and relevant flows

## Proposed Design
- High-level approach and justification
- Affected domains, services, projects, and endpoints
- Data model or contract changes (including versioning/compatibility strategy)
- External dependencies (jobs, infrastructure, third-party systems)

## Risks and Tradeoffs
- Key risks, open questions, and alternatives considered

## Testing and Validation
- Unit test strategy (including new or updated tests)
- Integration/end-to-end testing considerations
- Manual QA/UAT notes and linkage to UAT instructions if applicable

## Deployment, Rollback, and Observability
- Deployment or migration steps
- Rollback approach if needed
- Telemetry, logging, and alerting changes
          </format>
        </when>
        <when condition="work-item-is-story-or-medium-change">
          <format>
## Context and Goal
- One or two sentences describing the behavior change and why

## Design
- Where in the code this will be implemented (projects, services, helpers)
- Any contract or schema changes (if any)

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests to add or update
          </format>
        </when>
        <when condition="work-item-is-chore-or-small-change">
          <format>
## Plan
- Type: Chore / small change
- Exact method(s) or file(s) to touch (e.g., `ActionItemNotificationsHelperService.GenerateEventNotificationData`)
- One-sentence description of the new behavior or field

## Testing
- Minimal tests or spot checks required (if any)
          </format>
        </when>
      </rule>

      <step order="10">Bug-specific planning: Data Fix and Regression Prevention</step>
      <when condition="work-item-is-a-bug">

        <substep>Assess whether a data fix is necessary.</substep>
        <action>Determine if the bug caused corrupted, missing, or incorrect data that needs to be corrected independently of the code fix.</action>
        <action>If a data fix is needed, write the assessment to the `Custom.DataFix` field. If you can determine the actual SQL script for the fix, include it. Otherwise, describe what needs to be corrected and which tables/entities are affected.</action>
        <action>Use TWO operations in the updates array:</action>
        <format>
          [
            {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/Custom.DataFix", "value": "{data fix description with explicit \\n and SQL if known}"},
            {"op": "add", "path": "/multilineFieldsFormat/Custom.DataFix", "value": "Markdown"}
          ]
        </format>
        <action>If no data fix is needed, write "No data fix required — the bug did not cause persistent data corruption." to the field.</action>

        <substep>Specify regression prevention measures.</substep>
        <action>Identify what new unit tests, integration tests, or acceptance tests should be added to prevent this bug from recurring.</action>
        <action>Be specific: name the test class or method pattern, the scenario being tested, and the expected vs. incorrect behavior.</action>
        <action>Write the assessment to the `Custom.RegressionPrevention` field:</action>
        <format>
          [
            {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/Custom.RegressionPrevention", "value": "{regression prevention details with explicit \\n}"},
            {"op": "add", "path": "/multilineFieldsFormat/Custom.RegressionPrevention", "value": "Markdown"}
          ]
        </format>

        <note>These fields should also be summarized within the technical plan itself so the full picture is in one place.</note>
      </when>

      <step order="11">Assess agent executability of the planned work</step>
      <action>Evaluate each component of the technical plan and determine whether it could be executed by an agent in a future automated flow.</action>
      <action>Append the following section to the end of the technical plan:</action>
      <format>
## Agent Executability Assessment

**Overall:** {Fully executable | Partially executable | Not executable}

**Executable by agent:**
- {task description}: {why — e.g., mechanical guard clause addition, well-defined pattern, existing test patterns to follow}

**Requires human:**
- {task description}: {why — e.g., needs product decision, touches auth/security, requires manual testing, design judgment needed}

**Prerequisites for agent execution:**
- {any blockers that must be resolved first — e.g., "data fix must be applied and verified", "team lead must confirm approach", "dependent PR must merge first"}
      </format>
      <rule description="Criteria for evaluating agent executability of each task">
        <action>**Code changes:** Is the fix mechanical and pattern-based (add a null check, add a guard clause, add a lock, add a filter, wire a new field through existing layers)? Or does it require design judgment, new architectural patterns, or product decisions?</action>
        <action>**Test changes:** Are there existing tests in the same file/project that demonstrate the pattern to follow? Or do tests need to be designed from scratch for a novel scenario?</action>
        <action>**Data fixes:** Script authoring is agent-executable, but script execution against production is NOT — always mark as "requires human" for execution.</action>
        <action>**Config or infrastructure changes:** Generally requires human unless the change is a simple, well-documented settings update.</action>
        <action>**Scope:** Contained changes (1-3 files, single service) are stronger candidates than cross-cutting changes touching many surfaces.</action>
      </rule>
      <note>This assessment is a handoff contract for a future agent execution flow. Be honest about limitations — overestimating executability wastes more time than underestimating it.</note>

      <step order="12">Determine Architecture Review status</step>
      <action>Evaluate the technical plan against the Architecture Review criteria to determine whether an architecture review is needed.</action>
      <rule description="Architecture Review criteria (from wiki)">
        <requirement>Set `Custom.ArchReviewStatus` to "Requested" if the work item meets ONE OR MORE of the following criteria:</requirement>
        <criteria>
          <item>**New Systems or Dependencies** — Introduction of a new project, repository, or service; adoption of a new technology stack, framework, or third-party service.</item>
          <item>**Data and Storage** — Creation or modification of a database, table, schema, or data model; ANY schema change to JSON models (NoSQL or JSON columns in SQL).</item>
          <item>**Infrastructure and Deployment** — Deployment of new infrastructure (servers, clusters, cloud resources); changes to existing infrastructure or deployment pipelines.</item>
          <item>**Core Functionality** — Changes that affect key business services (e.g., Charginator, Prescribinator, Action Items); modifications to shared libraries or APIs consumed by multiple services.</item>
          <item>**User Journeys and Experience** — Alterations to critical user flows (conversion funnels, checkout, onboarding); reordering, addition, or removal of key user events; clinician/provider experience workflow changes.</item>
          <item>**Business Impact** — Updates that introduce significant changes to customer or operational workflows.</item>
          <item>**Performance and Scale** — Projects that could impact performance benchmarks or introduce scaling concerns.</item>
          <item>**Security and Compliance** — Changes involving sensitive data or affecting regulatory compliance (GDPR, HIPAA).</item>
          <item>**Cost Implications** — High-cost infrastructure or operational expenses tied to new initiatives.</item>
        </criteria>
        <requirement>Set `Custom.ArchReviewStatus` to "Not Needed" if NONE of the above criteria apply.</requirement>
      </rule>
      <action>Use `mcp__ado__wit_update_work_item` to set the field:</action>
      <format>
        [
          {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/Custom.ArchReviewStatus", "value": "{Not Needed | Requested}"}
        ]
      </format>
      <action>Include the rationale in the technical plan under a brief `## Architecture Review` section at the end, stating which criteria triggered the review or why none apply.</action>
      <note>Valid values for `Custom.ArchReviewStatus` are: "Not Needed", "Requested", "In Progress", "Complete". The agent should only ever set "Not Needed" or "Requested". "In Progress" and "Complete" are managed by the architecture team after the review session.</note>
      <note>When set to "Requested", the system automatically generates an architecture review work item. The responsible engineer or team lead must then schedule the review for a Monday or Thursday architecture review session no later than the sprint before development begins.</note>

      <step order="13">Review the technical plan with the user</step>
      <action>Present the technical plan in a concise, digestible format and ask the user to confirm or request changes</action>
      <requirement>Do NOT proceed to implementation until the user confirms the technical plan is acceptable</requirement>

      <step order="14">Run the Agent Confidence Assessment</step>
      <action>Execute the Agent Confidence Assessment workflow defined in <see id="agent-confidence-location"/></action>
      <action>Append the resulting assessment to the end of the technical plan before posting it to the work item</action>
      <note>This step evaluates the overall work item for agent readiness, complementing the more granular Agent Executability Assessment produced in step 11</note>

      <step order="15">Post the confirmed technical plan to the work item</step>
      <action>After the user confirms the plan, use `mcp__ado__wit_update_work_item` to write the plan to the `Custom.TechnicalPlan` field</action>
      <rule description="Writing the technical plan as markdown to the work item">
        <requirement>Include TWO operations in the updates array to ensure ADO renders the field as Markdown</requirement>
        <format>
          [
            {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/Custom.TechnicalPlan", "value": "{the confirmed technical plan with explicit \\n after every line and \\n\\n between sections}"},
            {"op": "add", "path": "/multilineFieldsFormat/Custom.TechnicalPlan", "value": "Markdown"}
          ]
        </format>
      </rule>
      <action>Add the `agent-tech-planned` tag to the work item (append to existing `System.Tags` if not already present). If a data fix script was also written to `Custom.DataFix`, add the `agent-data-fix-scripted` tag.</action>
      <action>Post an AGENT comment noting the technical plan was saved to the work item</action>

    </workflow>

  </section>

  <!-- Stack-Specific Planning Guidance -->
  <section description="Additional planning context for work items targeting specific technology stacks">

    <rule description="Detecting which stack a work item targets">
      <trigger>Drafting a technical plan for any work item</trigger>
      <action>Determine the target stack from the work item's Area Path, description, and related code:</action>
      <action>- Area paths under `Products\EMR`, `Infrastructure`, `Architecture`, `DAML`, or `Change Management` → Backend (.NET/C#)</action>
      <action>- Area paths under `Products\Patient Exp` or mentioning the patient app, patient-experience repo, or Next.js components → Frontend (Next.js/React)</action>
      <action>- Area paths under `Products\Public Sites` → May be frontend (checkout flows) or mixed</action>
      <action>- Some work items span both stacks (e.g., new API endpoint + patient app UI). In that case, the technical plan should address both stacks.</action>
    </rule>

    <sub-section description="Patient Experience frontend stack context for technical planning">

      <rule description="Patient Experience application technology and architecture overview">
        <note>The patient experience application is a Next.js/React app hosted on Azure App Service via Docker. Source code lives in the `honehealth/patient-experience` GitHub repo (separate from the Time Therapeutics ADO repo).</note>
        <architecture>
          <item key="framework">Next.js with React</item>
          <item key="package-manager">pnpm (monorepo with packages)</item>
          <item key="hosting">Azure App Service (Docker container) — production at app.honehealth.com, dev at app.honehealthdev.com</item>
          <item key="preview-environments">Vercel spins up isolated preview environments per PR for testing</item>
          <item key="auth">Okta (preview environments require trusted origin configuration)</item>
          <item key="analytics">Fullstory for session replays, Application Insights for telemetry (cloud role name: HonePatientApp)</item>
          <item key="visual-regression">Chromatic for UI visual regression testing</item>
          <item key="linting">Prettier (auto-format via VS Code extension); build will fail if lint issues exist — run `pnpm -r build` then `npm run lint:fix` if needed</item>
          <item key="config">Environment variables via `.env.local` (local), Vercel dashboard (preview), Azure App Configuration with PWA: prefix (dev), pipeline variables (prod). NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix exposes vars to the browser — verify safety before using.</item>
          <item key="click-tracking">Custom click tracking via clickTrackingConstants.ts, integrated with Google Sheets mapping and App Insights customEvents</item>
          <item key="pii-masking">Fullstory integration requires explicit PII masking using Mask/Exclude components on any screen displaying patient data</item>
          <item key="source-control">GitHub (not ADO) — PRs created in GitHub, CI/CD pipelines in ADO</item>
          <item key="release-cadence">Dev deploys on merge to main; prod releases weekly on Tuesdays via semantic version tags (release/vX.Y.Z)</item>
        </architecture>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Frontend-specific technical plan considerations">
        <trigger>Technical plan targets the patient experience frontend</trigger>
        <requirement>When the plan involves frontend changes, address these additional areas in the Design section:</requirement>
        <action>- **Component location:** Identify which package and directory within the monorepo the change belongs to (e.g., `packages/ssr/src/...`)</action>
        <action>- **Server vs client rendering:** Specify whether the affected components are server-rendered (RSC), client-rendered (`'use client'`), or mixed, and whether the change affects that boundary</action>
        <action>- **API integration:** If the change consumes backend API endpoints, identify the endpoint(s), request/response contracts, and how errors are handled</action>
        <action>- **State management:** Note how state flows through the affected components (props, context, hooks, URL params)</action>
        <action>- **PII and Fullstory masking:** If the change adds or modifies screens displaying patient data, note that Fullstory Mask/Exclude components must be applied and session replays must be verified post-deploy</action>
        <action>- **Environment variables:** If new env vars are needed, document the variable name, which environments need it, and whether it uses the NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix (browser-exposed) or is server-only</action>
        <requirement>In the Testing section, address:</requirement>
        <action>- **Chromatic visual regression:** Note if the change affects existing component snapshots and whether visual diff approval will be needed</action>
        <action>- **Preview environment testing:** Specify what should be verified in the Vercel preview environment before merging</action>
        <action>- **Cross-browser / responsive considerations:** If the change affects layout or interaction patterns, note any browser or viewport-specific concerns</action>
        <requirement>In the Deployment section, address:</requirement>
        <action>- **Merge-to-main deploys to dev automatically** — note any dev verification steps</action>
        <action>- **Prod requires a release tag** — note if the change should go out in the next Tuesday release or needs an expedited push</action>
        <action>- **Revert strategy:** If issues are found in dev, the expectation is to revert the PR to keep main clean rather than hotfix forward</action>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Frontend-specific agent executability criteria">
        <trigger>Assessing agent executability for patient experience frontend work</trigger>
        <action>In addition to the general criteria in step 11, evaluate:</action>
        <action>- **React component changes:** Adding props, modifying conditional rendering, or wiring new data to existing components is generally agent-executable. Creating new page layouts, complex animations, or novel interaction patterns typically requires human design judgment.</action>
        <action>- **Styling changes:** Mechanical styling updates (spacing, colors mapped to tokens, responsive breakpoints following existing patterns) are agent-executable. Visual design decisions, new component aesthetics, or brand-sensitive changes require human review.</action>
        <action>- **API consumption:** Wiring a new or modified backend endpoint to an existing data-fetching pattern (e.g., existing hook/service pattern in the codebase) is agent-executable. Designing new data-fetching strategies or error-handling UX is not.</action>
        <action>- **Environment variable changes:** Adding a new env var following the documented pattern is agent-scriptable, but the actual provisioning in Vercel/Azure App Config/pipelines requires human action.</action>
        <action>- **Fullstory/PII masking:** Adding Mask/Exclude wrappers to new PII-displaying components is agent-executable, but verifying session replays in Fullstory requires human action.</action>
        <action>- **Visual regression (Chromatic):** The agent cannot approve Chromatic visual diffs — always mark this as a human prerequisite when component changes are involved.</action>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

</document>

<document description="Azure DevOps MCP tool standards for work items, wikis, and ADO-specific workflows">

  <!-- Initialization -->
  <section description="Initialization for Azure DevOps operations">

    <rule description="Prevents unnecessary project listing calls">
      <requirement>NEVER call `core_list_projects` - always use <see id="ado-project-name" optional="true"/></requirement>
      <requirement>NEVER use the `repoNameFilter` parameter, it is not required</requirement>
      <requirement>When running `ado/wit_get_work_item`, do not filter fields, this can cause more errors than it is worth</requirement>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Work Items -->
  <section description="Work item field paths, states, and operations">

    <sub-section description="Work item identification from branch names">

      <rule description="Extracts work item ID from git branch naming convention">
        <trigger>Need to identify the current work item</trigger>
        <requirement>Extract work item ID from the git branch name</requirement>
        <format>{prefix}/wi-{work_item_id}</format>
        <workflow description="Steps to extract work item ID from branch name">
          <step order="1">Get current branch name (e.g., `plat/wi-16879`)</step>
          <step order="2">Extract digits after `wi-` (e.g., `16879`)</step>
          <step order="3">Use this ID for work item operations</step>
        </workflow>
        <examples>
          <example>Branch: feature/wi-12345 -> Work Item ID: 12345</example>
          <example>Branch: plat/wi-16879 -> Work Item ID: 16879</example>
        </examples>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

    <sub-section description="Comment formatting for agent-generated updates">

      <rule description="Requires AGENT prefix with compact agent tag on all agent-generated comments">
        <requirement>ALL agent-generated comments MUST begin with `AGENT:` followed by a compact agent tag that does not render as a Markdown header.</requirement>
        <format>AGENT: [agent:{model-name}] {your comment content}</format>
        <examples>
          <example>AGENT: [agent:claude-sonnet-4-6] Completed implementation of the user authentication flow. Ready for code review.</example>
        </examples>
      </rule>

      <rule description="HTML structure for work item comments">
        <requirement>Format all work item comments as HTML. ADO comments do not reliably render markdown, so always use HTML tags for structure.</requirement>
        <requirement>Use <div>, <b>, <ul>, <li>, <br> tags for headings, bold text, bullet lists, and line breaks.</requirement>
        <format>
AGENT: [agent:{model-name}] <div><b>{Summary heading}</b><ul><li>{Bullet point 1}</li><li>{Bullet point 2}</li></ul><div>{Additional context if needed}</div></div>
        </format>
        <examples>
          <example>AGENT: [agent:claude-sonnet-4-6] <div><b>Implementation Complete</b><ul><li>Added validation logic to the order service</li><li>Updated unit tests</li></ul></div></example>
          <anti-pattern reason="Markdown does not render reliably in ADO comments">Using ## headings, - bullet lists, or **bold** markdown syntax in work item comments</anti-pattern>
        </examples>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Proper @mention format for HTML comments via API">
        <requirement>Use the data-vss-mention HTML attribute for user mentions to trigger notifications</requirement>
        <format><a href="#" data-vss-mention="version:2.0,{identity_id}">@{display_name}</a></format>
        <examples>
          <example><a href="#" data-vss-mention="version:2.0,c5b69412-39f5-688a-ba61-552dfa0405c3">@Nicholas Pirollo</a></example>
        </examples>
        <note>Plain text like `@<guid>` does NOT resolve to a clickable mention - must use the HTML anchor format</note>
        <note>The identity_id is retrieved via `mcp__ado__core_get_identity_ids` tool</note>
      </rule>

      <rule id="ado-markdown-field-formatting" description="Use markdown-style structure and explicit newlines for ADO long-text fields">
        <requirement>When writing to any long-text Azure DevOps field (Release Notes, UAT Instructions, Agent Findings, Technical Plan, Data Fix, Regression Prevention, etc.) via `wit_update_work_item`, structure the content as markdown with clear headings and bullet lists.</requirement>
        <requirement>Always include explicit \n newline characters after every line and \n\n between logical sections; do not rely on automatic wrapping.</requirement>
        <requirement>Always include at least one trailing \n at the very end of the value string.</requirement>
        <requirement>Always include a second patch operation in the `updates` array to set the field format to Markdown, preventing ADO from defaulting to HTML rendering.</requirement>
        <format>
{"op": "add", "path": "/fields/{fieldPath}", "value": "## Heading\n- bullet\n\n"},
{"op": "add", "path": "/multilineFieldsFormat/{fieldName}", "value": "Markdown"}
        </format>
        <examples>
          <example>fieldPath: `Custom.AgentFindings`, fieldName: `Custom.AgentFindings`</example>
          <example>fieldPath: `Custom.TechnicalPlan`, fieldName: `Custom.TechnicalPlan`</example>
          <example>fieldPath: `Custom.UATReleaseNotes`, fieldName: `Custom.UATReleaseNotes`</example>
        </examples>
        <note>This rule applies to work item **fields** updated via `wit_update_work_item`. Work item **comments** use HTML (not markdown) — see the HTML comment formatting rule above.</note>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

  <!-- Pull Requests -->
  <section description="Pull request comment and thread operations">

    <sub-section description="Line-specific PR comment requirements">

      <rule description="Required parameters for line-specific PR thread comments">
        <trigger>Creating a comment thread on a specific line in a PR using `mcp__ado__repo_create_pull_request_thread`</trigger>
        <requirement>When specifying `filePath` and `rightFileStartLine`, you MUST also provide offset parameters</requirement>
        <requirement>`rightFileStartOffset` must be >= 1 (cannot be 0 or omitted)</requirement>
        <requirement>`rightFileEndOffset` should be set to approximate end of the line content</requirement>
        <format>
          filePath: "/path/to/file.cs"
          rightFileStartLine: {line_number}
          rightFileStartOffset: 1
          rightFileEndLine: {line_number}
          rightFileEndOffset: {approximate_line_length}
        </format>
        <examples>
          <example>rightFileStartOffset: 1, rightFileEndOffset: 40 - for a typical code line</example>
          <anti-pattern reason="API error - offset cannot be 0">Omitting offset parameters when filePath is specified</anti-pattern>
          <anti-pattern reason="API error - offset cannot be 0">Setting rightFileStartOffset: 0</anti-pattern>
        </examples>
        <note>Omitting filePath creates a general PR comment (not line-specific) - offsets not needed in that case</note>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

  <!-- Wiki -->
  <section description="Wiki and documentation lookup procedures">

    <sub-section description="Specification document lookup workflow">

      <rule description="Process for finding linked wiki documentation for work items">
        <trigger>Starting work on a feature or epic</trigger>
        <requirement>Search the Azure DevOps Wiki for linked specification documents</requirement>
        <workflow description="Steps to find specification documents linked to work items">
          <step order="1">Get the work item details using `mcp__ado__wit_get_work_item` with expand="relations"</step>
          <step order="2">Check for wiki page links in the work item relations</step>
          <step order="3">If no direct link, check the parent work item for wiki links</step>
          <step order="4">Search the wiki using `mcp__ado__search_wiki` with the feature/epic name if no direct links found</step>
          <when condition="wiki-page-found">
            <step>Retrieve wiki content using `mcp__ado__wiki_get_page_content`</step>
            <step>Use the spec doc context to inform implementation decisions</step>
          </when>
        </workflow>
        <rule description="Wiki identifier usage for direct wiki operations">
          <requirement>When calling wiki list or page APIs that require a wiki identifier, always use <see id="ado-wiki-identifier" optional="true"/> as the `wikiIdentifier`.</requirement>
          <note>For known pages like the General-Developer's-Handbook or Architecture-Reference, prefer calling `mcp__ado__wiki_get_page_content` with the full `url` value provided by constants rather than trying to discover them via listing.</note>
        </rule>
        <note>The "Spec Docs" section in the wiki contains requirements documents that drove backlog items</note>
        <note>Each wiki spec is linked to the relevant feature and/or epic</note>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Search patterns for finding related wiki documentation">
        <trigger>Need to find related documentation</trigger>
        <examples>
          <example>Feature work item 12345 -> Search wiki for "12345" or feature title</example>
          <example>Epic "User Authentication" -> Search wiki for "User Authentication" or "Authentication Spec"</example>
          <example>Check parent epic for broader context when working on child tasks</example>
        </examples>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

  <!-- Workflows -->
  <section description="ADO-specific workflows for common operations">

    <sub-section description="End-of-day update workflow">

      <workflow description="Process for posting end-of-day progress updates to work items">
        <trigger>Asked to provide an EOD update or general progress update</trigger>
        <step order="1">Identify work item ID from current branch name</step>
        <step order="2">Fetch commits for today's date on the current branch using git log with date filtering or ADO commit search (if git log fails to find changes)</step>
        <step order="3">Generate summary comment - keep it high-level and concise, focus on accomplishments not implementation details</step>
        <step order="4">Check for Product Owner/Requested By field on the work item</step>
        <when condition="work-item-has-product-owner-or-requested-by">
          <step>Search for that user to retrieve their ADO identity ID</step>
          <step>Append a CC mention at the bottom of the comment</step>
        </when>
        <rule description="Format for end-of-day update comments">
          <format>
AGENT: [agent:{model-name}] <div><b>Update</b> — {High-level summary of work completed today}<br/><br/>CC: <a href="#" data-vss-mention="version:2.0,{identity_id}">@{display_name}</a></div>
          </format>
          <requirement>ALWAYS include a <br/><br/> between the summary and the CC mention</requirement>
          <anti-pattern reason="missing line break">AGENT: Update - Summary here. CC: @Name</anti-pattern>
          <note>The @mention must remain as an HTML anchor tag — this is required by the ADO API for triggering notifications.</note>
        </rule>
      </workflow>

    </sub-section>

    <sub-section description="Agent findings summary workflow">

      <workflow description="Process for recording agentic investigation findings into the Agent Findings field">
        <trigger>Agent completes a triage, investigation, or analysis for a work item — including cases where the work item is determined not to be a candidate for the workflow</trigger>
        <step order="1">Ensure a concise, markdown-structured summary of findings is prepared, with clear headings, bullet lists, and blank lines between sections.</step>
        <step order="2">Write this summary to the `Custom.AgentFindings` field using `/fields/Custom.AgentFindings` as the field path.</step>
        <step order="3">Format the field update following the markdown field formatting rule (see rule id="ado-markdown-field-formatting" above).</step>
        <step order="4">After updating `Custom.AgentFindings`, post an AGENT-formatted HTML comment on the work item summarizing that findings have been updated and pointing readers to the Agent Findings field.</step>
        <rule description="Recommended markdown structure for Agent Findings">
          <format>
TL;DR (Bottom Line)
- Verdict: {Bug / Expected behavior / Inconclusive - short reason}
- Key conclusion: {One-sentence, plain-language statement of the most important finding}
- Primary next step: {One-sentence action + owner type (e.g., "Backend engineer: ...")}

Summary
- {One or two sentences describing the main conclusion}

Evidence
- History: {Whether history data was queried; if yes, which entities/tables/fields and time windows; if no, why not}
- Telemetry: {Whether telemetry was queried; if yes, which operations/services and time windows; if no, why not}
- {Key observations or metrics that support the conclusion}

Code & Change History
- {Inferred repository/system/area/component/method, or a clear statement that it could not be determined}
- {Recent code or configuration changes in that area, or a note that none were identified}

Non-Findings
- {Important checks that showed no issues}

Impact
- {Scope and severity of the issue}

Next Steps
- {Recommended actions or follow-up work}
          </format>
          <note>This structure is a guideline; adapt section names when appropriate, but always include a TL;DR (Bottom Line) section at the top and preserve clear headings and bullet lists with newlines.</note>
        </rule>
      </workflow>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

</document>

<document description="Git MCP tool standards for version control operations including commits, branches, and PRs">

  <!-- Initialization -->
  <section description="Initialization requirements for git operations">

    <rule description="Ensures working directory is set before any git operation">
      <trigger>Any git command needs to be ran</trigger>
      <requirement>Set the current working directory as the working directory with a tool call to `git_set_working_dir`</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Mandates use of MCP git tools over bash commands">
      <requirement>Always use MCP git tools (mcp__git__*) instead of bash git commands</requirement>
      <note>MCP git tools provide better integration, error handling, and structured output</note>
      <examples>
        <example>To view uncommitted changes, use an MCP tool call instead of `git diff`</example>
        <example>To view commit history, use an MCP tool call instead of `git log`</example>
        <example>To show a specific commit or file at a revision, use an MCP tool call instead of `git show`</example>
        <anti-pattern reason="use MCP tool">Running `git status` via Bash tool</anti-pattern>
        <anti-pattern reason="use MCP tool">Running `git diff` via Bash tool</anti-pattern>
        <anti-pattern reason="use MCP tool">Running `git log` via Bash tool</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Commit Standards -->
  <section description="Commit message formatting and authorization rules">

    <rule description="Defines the required format for commit messages including work item prefix">
      <requirement>All commit messages MUST include the work item number as a prefix</requirement>
      <format>#{work_item_id} {descriptive message}</format>
      <note>From branch `plat/wi-1300`, use prefix `#1300`</note>
      <format>
        Header: #{work_item_id} {imperative verb} {description}

        Body (optional):
        - Explain WHY the change was made, not just WHAT
        - Keep lines under 74 characters
        - Reference related work items if applicable
      </format>
      <examples>
        <example>#1300 Add user authentication service</example>
        <example>
          #1234 Fix notification scheduling issue

          Updates the notification scheduler to correctly handle edge cases
          where a user's timezone changes. Previously, notifications could be missed.
        </example>
        <anti-pattern reason="missing work item prefix">Add user authentication service</anti-pattern>
        <anti-pattern reason="wrong format">WI-1300 Add user authentication service</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Prevents unauthorized commits without explicit user approval">
      <requirement>NEVER commit code unless explicitly told to do so</requirement>
      <note>This is critical - wait for explicit user approval before any commit</note>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Branch Standards -->
  <section description="Branch naming conventions and standards">

    <rule description="Defines the branch naming format including work item ID">
      <requirement>Branch names follow a standard format that includes the work item ID</requirement>
      <format>{prefix}/wi-{work_item_id}</format>
      <examples>
        <example>feature/wi-12345</example>
        <example>plat/wi-16879</example>
        <example>bugfix/wi-9001</example>
      </examples>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Pull Request Standards -->
  <section description="Pull request checklist and validation requirements">

    <rule description="Checklist to verify before creating or approving a PR">
      <trigger>Before creating or approving a PR</trigger>
      <requirement>Verify all checklist items</requirement>
      <format>
        - Link the ADO work item in PR description
        - Use commit header format `#{work_item_id} - ...`
        - Build succeeds: `dotnet build <see id="solution-file"/>`
        - Tests pass: `dotnet test <see id="solution-file"/>`
        - No unintended changes: review diff for large renames or generated artifacts
        - Configuration stays local: secrets/config not committed; use `appsettings.Development.json`
        - CI green: PR validation jobs pass
      </format>
    </rule>

  </section>

</document>
